# KI-Arbeitspartner - an AI working partner, backed by a personal "KI-Betriebssystem" (AI operating system)

> Concept page for Henning Halfpap's AI working partner: an AI that works with
> his knowledge, his processes and his rules. Behind the customer-facing German
> term "KI-Arbeitspartner" (AI working partner) sits the category term
> "KI-Betriebssystem" (AI operating system). Private project, in daily
> operation since March 2026, grown from a 769-byte nucleus. This page shows
> the concept, not the internals - the repository stays private.

## What it is

Not software you install, but the layer between human and AI model. The model
provides the intelligence; this layer turns it into a working partner that
knows and keeps your context:

- Knowledge: a structured store (projects, areas, references) with a "map
  file" as the entry point - a map, not an encyclopedia.
- Coordination: recurring workflows as commands - session start with a
  situation picture, planning, review, clean daily shutdown.
- Learning: mistakes and insights become protocol entries and rules instead
  of repeating themselves.
- Maintenance: the system de-bloats itself on a cadence - archive, condense,
  measure.
- Integration (cross-cutting): connection to the real environment via open
  standards, so answers become actions.

## What it does today (four examples from live operation)

- Memory architecture: daily logs, learning protocol and long-term memory are
  layered so every session starts with context instead of from zero.
- Built-in maintenance layer: a monthly /monthly run de-bloats, archives and
  measures - the most-forgotten layer of such systems.
- Credential governance: secrets live in a keychain, never in plain text,
  never in the knowledge store; clear rules govern which access is used where
  and how it is handed over.
- Homelab & MCP integration: the partner talks to the real environment
  (document archive, smart home, monitoring, network) via the open Model
  Context Protocol; three of those integrations are self-built and public.

## Genesis

March 2026: a 769-byte map file (24 lines) plus a handful of command stubs
from an AI training course. Three and a half months of daily operation later:
850+ structured knowledge files, own skills and MCP servers, hooks and
automations, a lived learning protocol, and a maintenance layer that keeps
growth in check. Four waves: Foundation (April), Integration (May), Learning +
Maintenance (June), Consolidation & visibility (July). Honest flip side:
growth creates maintenance debt; the system documents it and pays it down
deliberately.

## What it is NOT

- Not a product: no download, no subscription, no course.
- Not a chatbot or a prompt trick - the difference is the system around the AI:
  knowledge, rules, memory, maintenance.
- Not a blueprint to copy: the concept transfers, the copy does not.
- Not finished: a living system never is.

## Why it transfers

The base concept is generic. Anyone with their own knowledge and recurring
processes - a law firm with files and deadlines, a trade business with quotes
and measurements, a freelancer with clients and workflows - has the raw
material for such a partner. The core is plain text files and open standards
(agent skills, MCP); your knowledge stays yours, it does not belong to the
tool. This instance runs on Claude Code; the concept itself is tool-open.
